reviews.review_description:
I really want to like this game. I love big games with any semblance of a story, I love shooters I love swinging, but this game is just not hitting. I got it because it was $5 but honestly, I almost think it was a waste of $5 and that's sad because I can tell people put work into this. 
WHY DID YOU MAKE IT RIDICULOUSLY HARD TO KNOW WHERE TO GO I MEAN SERIOUSLY THAT WAS A MAP????
I just got to the place where it says I'm supposed to know where to go or something.... I have no idea where to go and I'm sick of looking it up I shouldn't have to look up YouTube videos to know where to go.

I'm sorry but the devs really let this one down.

I just started this a few days before writing this review. I'm nearing the end and will likely finish it today or tomorrow as I have time. I'm fairly busy between work, family, and life. I have tons of other games to play both in VR and flat. All that to say I loved it enough to skip playing anything else and spend my free time(and sleep time) on playing this.

Where to start:

Pros:
- Awesome soundtrack and intro that gets you pumped to play. Seriously the beginning title splash intro after the tutorial was really well done. You felt like you were an agent being orbital dropped into a nuclear wasteland i.e. exactly what you should feel. I've been humming the main theme since I started and it's used during the halfway point really well. The other tracks were less noticable.
- Great movement system that evolves even in the late game and you continue to get better at using it.
- Great environments, the draw distance and vistas are huge and it still runs smooth. No it's not 90 fps native. The detail is pretty good even if the textures can seem more simple. I generally liked it. The lighting is dynamic on you, your weapons, and vehicle. Sometimes the environmental lighting was surprisingly good. It usually runs good except for some loading areas and occasionally slow down.
- Related to the environments is the physics based elements. Enemy armor drops off after taking to many hits. Tons of stuff is setting around to bang around upon an explosion or gun fire though combat areas usually leave the physics stuff inside. The weapons have a nice physical feel to them.
- Upgrading default pistol(love it), new areas push the player to use their abilities more and more. Loved the late game combat movement mechanics.
- Side areas and even secret side areas with ammo and weapon rewards.
- Climbing, sliding, grappling, and wall running work well and fit naturally in most of the environment. The game doesn't even mark climbing locations yet 95% of the time when I grabbed for something I thought I should be able to grab it worked, a very intuitive climbing system.
- Great sequences, not to spoil to much but they have some escape sequences, rail sliding flipping wall running fights. Some great combat stuff that is held back by enemy scripting and weapon grabbing system.
- Lengthy campaign though it could use a few side characters, there was room here for side quests and an armor element.
- No need for HUD.

Cons:
- Enemy behavior, they get stuck and become easy two shot, they don't move or use abilities much at all. They will kind of move from cover to cover, but mostly just wait for you to poke out. They will occasionally flank or blitz you, but yeah it's pretty lame. You will still die plenty, but it's more because you grabbed for ammo and took out your empty default pistol instead.
- The weapon and ammo grabbing system, I never quite got used to it. I think the issue is for one they didn't have a good explanation for it and the second issue is 
- Terrible vehicle control. It WILL make some of you sick, maybe even with some of the comfort settings on. This is absolutely one of the mixed parts of the game. On one hand you are racing across these large open desert valleys and canyons, even kind of one hand driving and shooting. Then you have these terrible jump/ramp sections, extremely sensitive turning, misaligned view and it's honestly even worse with their setting to have it fixed to the vehicle direction....yeah just a mess of controls here.
- Environment fighting doesn't work well. Yes you
disarm with a point and pull, but grabbing a barrel and tossing it at an enemy? Good luck chuck.
- Boss battles are a bit of a hit and miss. Some moments they are legit amazing and others some laughable zipping around by the boss. The bosses needed lots of polish.
- I have to point out that it did crash about three times. No doubt game has bugs.
- Story/plot mostly just notes and voice overs. Some of the big moments should have had more direct impact on the player.

The game has unrealized potential. There was a few sections that could have went deeper into the horror aspect, but they didn't. The escape sequences could be more intense. An armor health element would have worked well, grabbing armor and tossing it on yourself to armor that particular part would have been a great halfway point game change up. Enemy behavior is yikes.

I still absolutely recommend it for VR FPS fans, wastepunk fans, and anyone who can put up with a bit of jank.

By all means the game is not perfect but if you considering buying this game I played a similar game called stride fates which is a lot more polished  but this one has a lot more passion poured into it you get really cool real time cutscenes and a kicka** soundtrack there are bugs to this game but that hasn't stopped me from doing a second playthrough there's also 6 endings im pretty sure so it has some replay value some stride fates doesn't have to much of love what you're doing gorapid pls keep up the good work

reviews.review_title: This game is peak but un optimized
reviews.review_description:
Please read this, this game is so good but it is BUGGY AS HELL AND DOES NOT HOLD YOUR HAND WHATSOEVER 

Most of the time of me playing this game was just me running or driving around FOR HOURS to find where the hell I am even supposed to go


FIX THIS
Genuinely a fantastic game to play loads of awesome set pieces a decent lengthy campaign fun combat the driving works well if you like FPS games you should definitely give this a try

Meta offered  a free trial so I tried it and immediately purchased it at the end of the trial.  It’s a super fun game. I played Half life 2 in VR, and The Burst has the same vibe. it’s an apocalyptic first person shooter. The guns are cool and the mechanics smooth. I have not experienced any glitches at all.ATT DEVS: I hope there’s a second game in development. PLEASE and thank you. ❤️❤️❤️👍👍👍🔥🔥🔥
This is a great game. My only concern is that it gets repetitive with the levels. The game could have been shorter. Less is more.

So yea the game is a bit janky. Mostly in the storage/retrieval of weapons. I'd prefer my pistol lower by my belt and the shoulder weapons take abit to get used to. 

However if you get past that this game is just a blast to play. From racing around on the bike to zipping through the air to shooting baddies with a massive arsenal of weapons. This game is always changing things up so you stay entertained throughout.
Get it if you can you'll love it.
I highly recommend this game to Mad Max/Borderlands fans of any kind. Plus the parkour is so unbelievably good, while there is a bit of jank but it's no where near game-breaking. 10/10

reviews.review_title: If I check my meta menu it shuts down
reviews.review_description: Can’t leave the game for a second or it’ll shut down causing me to lose progress

Very fun, very big Mad Max FPS. I wish there was a map. I wish it was easier to store guns on your back but all the things that were rough on launch have been fixed and the game is smooth and pretty polished right now. 14.99 for a big game like this is A STEAL! Web slinging and plinking with Fallout guns...also the best driving mechanics. Very well done

Wow, theres alot here and alot to say! This game itself is fantastic and worthy of 5 stars for anyone who doesn't mind minor bugs/glitches or a lack of the polish that you might find in AAA titles with a huge budget and production team behind it. This game brings so much to the table in a good length campaign that rewards exploration. There are areas that can be a little confusing on where to go as this game doesn't hold your hand much outside of the "tutorial" level ***note to devs: this game would benefit greatly from a map with objective markers*** as i said prior there are minor bugs that can break emersion (nothing serious or gamebreaking in my experience). The storyline, gameplay mechanics and just the fact that they managed to put so much into this game without feeling forced or crammed is an accomplishment in itself.there is so much good you can say about this game  that outweighs any negatives significantly, in my opinion. I dont know anything about the devs but this feels like one of those rare indy titles that outshines alot of larger studio games in many ways. All things considered;I do recommend this game

I got this because another game that had a terrible launch - bulletstorm- turned into one of the best games on quest. So i hoped this would be the same. So many people are saying it is so much better than it was. All I can say is that it must have been really bad. I crash the car, it doesn’t respawn I i have to restart and then I can’t slide under the door, which I did first time round but now I slide but I don’t go through. If the tutorial is this broken then the game must be a bag of bugs! As I now can’t progress past minute 2 of the game - it’s a refund for me.

reviews.review_title: Still in need of more polish.
reviews.review_description:
I've not got too far into the game yet but I'm already encountering potential show stopping gameplay issues.

First up, the game looks pretty good and has a very atmospheric Mad Max post apocalyptic desert style vibe. So far the game seems quite linear, but there are still plenty of nooks and crannies to explore. Gameplay so far is basically traversing the levels and shooting bad guys.

Plenty of options to adjust the game, and I really recommend switching the run to a stick press and the jump to a simple button press. Climbing and driving are reasonably good. Not the best you'll ever come across in VR but decent enough.

My main issues with the game so far are with the combat. The gun handling is not the best and they lack punch. There seems to be no haptics, so they feel insubstantial. Also the back holstering of weapons is extremely problematic. It's easy enough to holster a weapon over your shoulder but very finicky to retrieve, especially in combat, where it becomes almost impossible. There is a faint audio cue for when you have your hand in the right place to retrieve a weapon from over your shoulder, but in combat the noise of the music, gun shots etc, drown it out and make it inaudible. As there is no haptics buzz to accompany the audio cue, it just seems impossible to retrieve a shoulder holstered weapon in combat. This is a big problem in a combat focused game. If the grab spot over the shoulder was bigger, this probably wouldn't be an issue.

I shall try and persevere with it for a bit longer, but this could potentially kill the game for me.

reviews.review_title: It is such an amazing game
reviews.review_description: Such a good game very fun you know very good gunplay but just all you need to actually enjoy this game is just a bit of common sense cuz if you don't have that you're going to get stuck very quickly
This game is terrific. I just hav an issue with the game exiting when I take my headset off.

The games consept is wonderful the mechanics not so. I would say its not good for a 30 dollar game but would be great for a 25/20 dollar game it has lags but if you ignore them, its kinda hard not to, then you got a great madmax look alike game
I like the atmosphere and mechanics of this title. Also the campaign is interesting and keeps you wanting to know more about the world.

Just behinde asgards wrafth in graphics and game play for a fraction of the price, I played true the demo and bought the game straight away for 6$ on sale. Great bargon, could easy cost 35$ in the store.

this has more story, openness, gameplay and environment variety, and overall quality than most native quest games on the market. i know it's due to a shaky launch with bugs galore, but now it is one of the best games on quest. do yourself a favor and don't miss out on this. thank you devs for your efforts, games the most fun I've had in a while.

A
Very solid game. Good atmosphere. 
You get on your bike. You ride. You halt where you want. Look for ammunition.
A lot of fun. A good deal.
Thank you.
The gameplay is so fun and it actually encourages the use of multiple weapons I love it one petpeeve is how hard it is to grab my shoulder weapons but hey no games perfect worth the 14 and more

It takes very long to finish which i am very glad for. The movement and story is the best ive played on quest and I've been playing for over 4 years now. I would love a part 2 as the ending could be explored a little further.
Everything needs work.

It’s a good niche if you like this combo in vr 
-gunplay 
-parkour with climbing and grappling 
-ATV riding
This is a freaking masterpiece. This is what VR shooters should be like. It's a new standard that few Meta Quest games can live up to.

I got this game on offer for £4 and cannot believe the quality - I highly recommend this to any Mad Max/Borderlands fans. This plays better than Borderlands 2 VR!

There are a few little issues here and there - but they’re pretty minor and are outweighed by positives.

I’m not too far in to the story yet, but have high hopes for DLC stories/vehicles/weapons that might be on the horizon.

Massive well done to the developers for creating something so open world feeling on the headset.

I don't understand the hate,  this game is absolutely fabulous!!! I promise you!! Is it perfect no,  but by no means is it bad.  If you ever played mad max and thought to yourself this would be cool in vr.. look no further.  The running jumping and climbing is worth the price of admission itself

reviews.review_title: Shows promise, but too much jank.
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: still can't grab the revolver behind my back, and I also can't store anything in that slot now. Also, the grappling mechanics are just off. You can't always grapple onto the desired points (and really, you should be able to hrapple onto anything). All the aforementioned jank plus non-responsive developers equals refund. It's a bummer; I really wanted to like this game.

So far the premise seems pretty great, but I'm only in the tutorial and I've already encountered some jank. At the part where you shoot out the panels and find a red revolver-style gun, you are prompted to store it behind your back. After about a dozen tries of trying and failing, only to drop the gun in the sand behind me, I was finally able to store it on my back (I think). Now I'm not able to retrieve it no matter how far back I reach and grab.

I found the dev's website, but unfortunately, their link to the discord server doesn't work. All discord links that I've found in other online locations don't work either, and the dev hasn't responded to my email yet.

The graphics and textures are pretty rough and the jumping mechanism is ridulously convoluted, but otherwise it shows a lot of promise. I just hope the weapon storage thing doesn't end up being a game-breaker.

Alright. So I originally wrote a bad review saying the game was glitchy and laggy. 

But boy has that changed. 

I just finished the game, and it’s smoother, better, and way more kickass than ever before!!

The storyline is really really creative and interesting, and I really do hope for a sequel!!!

This is such an awesome and well thought out game. I mean the story got me really hooked, I cared about what's gonna happen to Damag. There's a really surprising amount of lore and it's not a lazy few notes written quickly, everything makes sense, good voice acting as well. Levels are cleverly designed and are making an impression of traversing an open world. I loved the graphics and through all the game I felt the amount of work and heart the developers had put into it. I can't believe how underrated this game is because RATATATING THEM STUPID BOOMBOYS IS SO FUN, MORE MEET FOR THE FEAST!!!
Fun

reviews.review_title: The best action game I've ever played!!!
reviews.review_description: When I was playing this I felt like I was mad max and spiderman combined. It makes you feel like you're in open world, it's got amazing weapons, a vehicle and a pistol you can upgrade. However there are some bugs in the like weapons not appearing in the logs, the sitting mode isn't working because when it up I've become half as tall. And a weapon not showing a shield. I hope you guys fix it.

The game is super fun with awesome gun designs, awesome action, Great parkour, and good characters and story. My one problem of this game is that grabbing a gun over my shoulder is very inconsistent sometimes I grab it some times I don’t. Overall a super underrated game that more people should check out.￼￼

reviews.review_title: Good, but need a mirrored controller use
reviews.review_description: Pretty good game. Some bugs appears but you can ignore them. My only issue, that im left handed and its pretty hard to move and aim and shoot at the same time :( I hope they will upgrade it to get a mirrored control mapping. Then ill give the game five stars cuz its a cool world with cool skills!

reviews.review_title: It will be epic after polish.
reviews.review_description: I like the game really. It has great mechanics and feels really good! Yes, it needs fixes and good polishing, but team is fully on it. I'm quite confident about it.

reviews.review_title: A great game and i have some ideas
reviews.review_description: i love you game a lot but i wished there was multiplayer and maybe other game modes like a arena or something like that.
I waited for updates before I bought it and now I can assure you that the game is ready and the gameplay is great.

I’ve been watching this game for a while and when it came out, I waited and glad I did, with the recent update in graphics and everything this game is a must have a must play

The story is very nice, voice acting good as hell, this game should be in everyone’s library

This game is fun, it’s for those who got vr legs, YouTube videos don’t show the beauty of being in a virtual world

Continue with this universe devs, expand on it, you guys just opened Pandora’s box, the possibilities are endless 

And I highly recommend this vr gem

I've been watching this game since development and was sad by the poor release. But I love the fixes and helping out new studios so I decided to try it out. I hope this ends up being a success for the dev team so you guys can keep making games! Also the Bhaptic support played a massive part in me buying the game so if you do make more games please include that 🙏. Anyways thanks devs

reviews.review_title: Why do I have to put up with a chase
reviews.review_description: I wasn’t even half way through the game and I already hate it because of the stupid chase level and all that hard work through countless enemies to come to a chase level that shouldn’t be in the game because those types of levels always suck instead of making a chase level why not have a cutscene where the bad guys get to the scientist and leave before the protagonist could do anything it’s so annoying because you put multiple characters on bikes it’s what just made the game unbearable to play please do something about the chase level it’s actually infuriating to deal with.

reviews.review_title: Great adventure game. The Ai needs work.
reviews.review_description:
This is a really fun traversal game that has some awesome moments. 

I love how you can latch onto your vehicle and drive off pretty seamlessly. The actual climbing mechanics and everything else feels great. 

The issue is once you get into combat the AI is kind of stupid. One minute they’ll be chasing around which is engaging, but occasionally they’ll just stand there, not get behind cover and let you shoot them in the head. 

it’s a really fun game though and I recommend anybody who likes adventure games to get it, but hopefully there are fixes to the AI coming in the future to make it a little more engaging and give you a reason to have to run around more.

The music is on .2. Really really good pieces of music in this game. 

It’ll get a five star rating if AI is improved.

reviews.review_title: I want it to be awesome
reviews.review_description: I am enjoying it a lot BUT, I am finding bugs, vehicle often gets stuck on a bolder and from there is either reload level or walk the desert. Belt needs a lot of adjustment, also back holsters. Half times it won’t grab the gun. I like supporting devs during development phase, but this game needs to be AAA title, mechanics are great, environment is fantastic, weapons are cool but until some mechanics with handling objects and guns are resolved to feel smooth I can’t say is quite there. Even grabbing a leader to climb up feels clunky sometimes. I have uninstalled it and plan to install back after some updates are made to fix issues. Really don’t want this to be another “it had potential but…” come on devs, is now just down to tumbe details, what separates a ok game from a great tittle. 4 Stars waiting for a 5th to be add on with future updates

(1 MONTH after release Update:

Game is still riddled with bugs.
My favorites
1. I need to grab My shoulder weapons exactly 5 times before the hand decides to grab the weapon.
2. Glitching through the Floor or Ceilings.
It's a classic but for a reason. Always fun to see the upcoming Map by floating through Walls.

Game COULD be VERY fun without the Bugs.)

The game has tons of bugs there are literal MISSING files. (Like no joke there are some files that these devs just forgot to add... WTF!) Everything feels janky and unfinished. The Story is incredibly boring. And much much more.

I am uploading a full playthrough of the game on my channel "BamirengVR" where I go into detail why this game is not worth buying right now.

You just need to know that the idea is fun but the product is unfinished. Just wait a few more months. Until we (the idiots who preordered the game) are done playtesting and bugreporting.

I don't understand all that criticism. Sure there's some small bugs, but once you get used to the controls the game is really fun, and level design is awesome.

Something I miss in the game is a lantern, cause there's some very dark rooms at some point. It will be great to have one.

Thanks for the game and keep the good work.

reviews.review_title: Needs Melee weapons and some bug fixes
reviews.review_description:
Honestly I'm doing my best to get into this game. But it seems like even compared to Stride  and Bonelab it's janky as hell. Various times I find myself unable to reliably thwip onto the many hook points throughout the world resulting in me constantly falling to my death. And many times it feels like the gap I'm crossing is too wide for me to make it close enough to reach the hook point. The fact that this didn't come with melee weapons is very disappointing. Like why is there an axe in an area where you can clearly grab it, but it's just a prop? If they add melee I really hope they take a page from Dungeons of Eternity in an effort to reduce the amount of jank we're getting in game. Some adjustments to lighting and graphical fidelity on the quest 3 would be nice as well. Lastly they need to at least give us the option to have a full body character instead of just hands. It's very weird to be floating through this story as just a pair of floating hands. This would also help with this weird holstering system for the weapons they have. The pistol is just somewhere inside my chest and the weapons on my back don't always go to my hand when I grab them. There's also this tendency for weapons to just not go into the back holster when I try to put my weapons away. 
I really hope the developer takes what I had to say into consideration. This game feels like it's just on the edge of being the best thing I've ever played. Just needs that last bit of polish in a few extra things here and there. Driving was surprisingly fun and didn't have much jank outside of getting stuck in things. (Thank God there's usually a second vehicle nearby)

reviews.review_title: Fun game but needs an update
reviews.review_description: So far I’ve played this game for a few hours. The environment is rich and I won’t complain to much about the ai. The bugs in the game can be very frustrating. Gripping a two handed weapon is hard to do and is awkward. The lack of a map makes getting around and finding objectives somewhat difficult. All and all I think this could be a great game if som things are fixed.i give this a 4 star rating in hopes there is an update

reviews.review_title: Surprised at how fun this game is!
reviews.review_description: I almost didn’t buy it because all the bad reviews. I am on the last mission now about 6 hours of playtime in and I love this game! The setting and parkour mixed with gun fights is so amazing! There are a few minor bugs but nothing major or game breaking. I highly recommend playing this game. If you need a referral add and message me!

reviews.review_title: The game is far from being finished.
reviews.review_description:
The PC version supposedly works well, but the Quest version is unacceptable.

I would've kept the game and waited for the developers to fix it before completing it, but they're just dodging the issues, so I'm done with it.

This is what they wrote: "It is true that we had some problems due to hardware limitations on Quest, but we are actively working on it and will be updating the game with many improvements."

The issue isn't the performance but rather the fact that the game simply doesn't work. Take the swing system, for example—on Quest, it's very difficult to manage. But the main reason I'm refunding the game is the placement of the pistol. You can see your gun out of the corner of your eye, but you can never actually reach it. If you try to look down, the pistol's position shifts too. It's ridiculous that a game is released in such a state nowadays.

This is what happens to developers who prioritize PC development and only later focus on Quest.

developer_response.body:
Hi there! We are very sorry to hear that your first of impression of The Burst was negative. :C
We are working on the first big patch and we will continue improving our game until its perfect!

I already reported these issues to our team, we'll see what we can do about it!
We would really appreciate it if you could share your feedback with us on our Discord channel!
Love, GoFixit Studio!

reviews.review_description:
The game has tons of bugs in it and there's a jump that no one can make.
Also, the main physics system of jumping and parkoring and using wires is not reliable at all.
You only get about 30 minutes in before you can't go any farther. Many after the village where you actually get real guns fighting enemies. There's one jump that is impossible to make.
Also, there's missing textures all over the place where you don't know, if there's something is there or if there aint.

There are definitely few flaws that could be easily fixed with few updates (like small glitches), but the most important part as semi-open world game with great mechanics and sense of scale is there!

It was a joy to play it and am back to game as it gets better and better the more I play it.

developer_response.body:
We are already cooking the first patch! We will get better! Promise!
Warmest regards, GoFixit Studio!

reviews.review_title: I would rather play Stride Fates.
reviews.review_description:
So I usually am a HUGE fan of games that involve parkour.  I actually really enjoyed Stride Fates even though that game is hated among the vr community.  If you have a game that is intense and fast paced but also gives me an ability to run on walls, do some platforming, and destroy waves of enemies… 9 times out of 10 I will have a good time… This unfortunately was the 1 out of 10 times I didn’t want to play it anymore.

First off, the game gives you ZERO guidance on what to do in a level to progress… For example, this game is supposed to be a fast paced platforming shooter, not Bonelab!  I mean honestly, you are in a train and you have to get out of it; there’s no threat, there’s no enemy to speak of (at least until you get off of the train) and how do you get out of it? The first thing I’m NOT going to think of IS SHOOTING AT THE CEILING AT A LITTLE CRACK THAT BLENDS IN WITH THE REST OF THE BLACK CEILING!!! Honestly, is it too much to ask for a little waypoint for you to pay attention to when you’re lost? Or hell, maybe a map? ESPECIALLY for when you escape the Leadlair and the character hardly gives you any indication that you’re heading in the right direction? 

That’s another problem… I was playing through the first level after the prologue.  I was patiently waiting for the game to gracefully fade to black once I beat the first boss… only to find out that the game just doesn’t ever stop for any reason… The game was trying to be like an open world because the destinations were connected by you driving to them. Again, no indication whatsoever. 

Oh yeah and another thing, the enemy ai is not polished at all, they hardly react to you doing anything and they take WAY too much bullets to die.  This might be challenging if this game had a hard mode and I was playing it.  But no, the game wanted to drag itself out for longer than it needed to be by having enemies take over 50 bullets to die, and have the level design be as confusing as Boneworks by barely giving you direction. 

You are not going to be able to play the game completely without noticing how bad the enemy ai is, especially considering how much this game loves to repeat itself, over and over and OVER AGAIN!!! You drive from point A to point B, you get into a shootout with some enemies, then go on a scavenger hunt for a lever to pull that isn’t clearly marked, again the game padding itself out, open up a gate to drive through, REPEAT!

When you grapple hook onto one of the buggies, the physics are wrong too.  There have been numerous times where I have gotten a buggy stuck in a bunch of rocks and I would use the grapples to pull it out, only for the buggy to strafe away from me. 

I have heard YouTubers say that the PCVR version is better… until I saw Mr Vr Voice do his impressions video on it earlier today… and needless to say he had the same reaction I had but his was focused on how the game was not polished , and HE played the PCVR version…  

It honestly hurts me to give this game a 1 star because I really thought this was going to be better than Stride Fates considering that there’s much more content.  Maybe the game will get updated and MAYBE it will be better but right now, coming from someone who has finished the game’s story, I stand by my rating.

Although this review is negative, I do believe that the game can grow on me, considering the controls are something that I am comfortable with and I cannot wait until the game gets patched so that some of the issues can get resolved. The developers have already addressed a lot of them which gives me nothing but good signs.

I would absolutely be willing to overlook ALL of the game’s problems and change my rating to 5 stars if there was a waypoint. That’s my biggest issue is the game doesn’t have a waypoint that you can turn on in the settings for where you need to go or what you have to do in the room you’re in.
To say it was jank is an understatement. Just don't
Don’t waste your time here just another rushed vr game that feels and looks horrible
i can be trusted with this power trust.

reviews.review_title: Refunding to try this on steam
reviews.review_description: This is terrible compared to the steam demo.

developer_response.body:
We are very sorry that we disappointed you!
Our team is already working on the big update.
With love, GoFixit Studio

reviews.review_title: I love this game so much
reviews.review_description: Before this game launched, I thought it would be a fun bad game. A game with bad voice acting, a dumb story, and janky vehicles. I always expected this game to have great movement and fun combat, but nothing really more. At launch I was very surprised that the game had great vehicles, good voice acting and a good story. Even at launch I loved this game so much even though it was pretty buggy and broken. But after the patches it's even better. The combat is great, especially after getting the upgrades. Before you get the upgrades it feels like a tactical shooter, but once you get the upgraded hsm it becomes a tactical movement shooter. The parkour is probably the best part of the game. I takes the movement from Stride Fates and improves upon it and adds new movement options. The level design is also on point. It feels like you're actually exploring a destroyed planet run by convicts. The atmosphere is also incredible. You feel so alone riding through vast open desserts with nothing but ruins around you. The music is also just incredible, especially the main theme. I've had the main theme stuck in my head since launch. The graphics aren't anything to write home about but the environments are such a cool spectacle. The most surprising thing is the story. The story is pretty good. A lot of good and meaningful plot twists and character dynamics, and the voice acting really makes this story something special. Another very surprising thing for me was the lore. I thought the lore would be lazily made lore but it instead of making me have more questions, it gave me answers and added to the story and gave context to it. The sound effects are also very good and immersive. Now there is negatives but for the most part they aren't anything huge. One thing is that the gunplay can feel very janky at times. I also hate that the syringes are on your wrists because you can't really grab them unless you're standing still. Also Forgepolis is very laggy on quest 2.

I just played for 45 mins and had a blast! Sure, there is a glitch here and there but not bad and not game breaking! The grappling is great and the gun play is not that bad!  Just being able to drive around and explore is tons of fun!  I love finding hidden things in the world! Totally worth it!

I was waiting for this release and I must say I'm not experiencing its intended vibe!
There is hope providing the developers give it what's needed to make this what it should have been on its release date. Needs more attention to atmosphere, bring it alive with some color.
Give the desert some beauty.  The patch made some big corrections !
I'm not going to lose hope just yet because this can be a Banger!!
